3) Department module ka proper CRUD/UI
Ab department module ko bhi proper bana dete hain.

departments/forms.py
from django import forms
from .models import Department

class DepartmentForm(forms.ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = Department
        fields = "__all__"
departments/views.py
from django.shortcuts import render, redirect, get_object_or_404
from django.contrib import messages
from .models import Department
from .forms import DepartmentForm

def department_list(request):
    departments = Department.objects.all().order_by("id")
    return render(request, "departments/department_list.html", {"departments": departments})

def add_department(request):
    if request.method == "POST":
        form = DepartmentForm(request.POST)
        if form.is_valid():
            form.save()
            messages.success(request, "Department added successfully.")
            return redirect("department_list")
    else:
        form = DepartmentForm()

    return render(request, "departments/add_department.html", {"form": form})

def edit_department(request, pk):
    department = get_object_or_404(Department, pk=pk)

    if request.method == "POST":
        form = DepartmentForm(request.POST, instance=department)
        if form.is_valid():
            form.save()
            messages.success(request, "Department updated successfully.")
            return redirect("department_list")
    else:
        form = DepartmentForm(instance=department)

    return render(request, "departments/edit_department.html", {"form": form})

def delete_department(request, pk):
    department = get_object_or_404(Department, pk=pk)

    if request.method == "POST":
        department.delete()
        messages.success(request, "Department deleted successfully.")
        return redirect("department_list")

    return render(request, "departments/delete_department.html", {"department": department})

def department_profile(request, pk):
    department = get_object_or_404(Department, pk=pk)
    return render(request, "departments/department_profile.html", {"department": department})
departments/urls.py
from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path("", views.department_list, name="department_list"),
    path("add/", views.add_department, name="add_department"),
    path("edit/<int:pk>/", views.edit_department, name="edit_department"),
    path("delete/<int:pk>/", views.delete_department, name="delete_department"),
    path("profile/<int:pk>/", views.department_profile, name="department_profile"),
]
config/urls.py me add karo
path("departments/", include("departments.urls")),
Templates banao
Folder:

templates/departments/

{% extends "base.html" %}

{% block content %}

{% if messages %}
    {% for message in messages %}

    {% endfor %}
{% endif %}

<div class="d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center mb-3">
    <h2 class="mb-0">Department List</h2>
    <a href="/departments/add/" class="btn btn-primary">Add Department</a>
</div>

<table class="table table-bordered table-striped">
    <thead class="table-dark">
        <tr>
            <th>ID</th>
            <th>Name</th>
            <th>Code</th>
            <th>Actions</th>
        </tr>
    </thead>
    <tbody>
        {% for department in departments %}
        <tr>
            <td>{{ department.id }}</td>
            <td>{{ department.name }}</td>
            <td>{{ department.code }}</td>
            <td>
                <a href="/departments/profile/{{ department.id }}/" class="btn btn-info btn-sm">View</a>
                <a href="/departments/edit/{{ department.id }}/" class="btn btn-warning btn-sm">Edit</a>
                <a href="/departments/delete/{{ department.id }}/" class="btn btn-danger btn-sm">Delete</a>
            </td>
        </tr>
        {% empty %}
        <tr>
            <td colspan="4" class="text-center">No departments found.</td>
        </tr>
        {% endfor %}
    </tbody>
</table>

from subjects.models import Subject
course_profile update karo
def course_profile(request, pk):
    course = get_object_or_404(Course, pk=pk)
    related_subjects = Subject.objects.filter(course=course).order_by("name")

    return render(request, "courses/course_profile.html", {
        "course": course,
        "related_subjects": related_subjects,
    })

Step 1 — departments/views.py update
from django.shortcuts import render, redirect, get_object_or_404
from django.contrib import messages
from django.core.paginator import Paginator
from django.db.models import Q
from .models import Department
from .forms import DepartmentForm
from courses.models import Course
from faculty.models import Faculty


def department_list(request):
    query = request.GET.get("q")

    if query:
        department_qs = Department.objects.filter(
            Q(name__icontains=query) |
            Q(code__icontains=query)
        ).order_by("id")
    else:
        department_qs = Department.objects.all().order_by("id")

    paginator = Paginator(department_qs, 5)
    page_number = request.GET.get("page")
    departments = paginator.get_page(page_number)

    return render(request, "departments/department_list.html", {"departments": departments})


def add_department(request):
    if request.method == "POST":
        form = DepartmentForm(request.POST)
        if form.is_valid():
            form.save()
            messages.success(request, "Department added successfully.")
            return redirect("department_list")
    else:
        form = DepartmentForm()

    return render(request, "departments/add_department.html", {"form": form})


def edit_department(request, pk):
    department = get_object_or_404(Department, pk=pk)

    if request.method == "POST":
        form = DepartmentForm(request.POST, instance=department)
        if form.is_valid():
            form.save()
            messages.success(request, "Department updated successfully.")
            return redirect("department_list")
    else:
        form = DepartmentForm(instance=department)

    return render(request, "departments/edit_department.html", {"form": form})


def delete_department(request, pk):
    department = get_object_or_404(Department, pk=pk)

    if request.method == "POST":
        department.delete()
        messages.success(request, "Department deleted successfully.")
        return redirect("department_list")

    return render(request, "departments/delete_department.html", {"department": department})


def department_profile(request, pk):
    department = get_object_or_404(Department, pk=pk)
    related_courses = Course.objects.filter(department=department).order_by("name")
    related_faculty = Faculty.objects.filter(department=department).order_by("name")

    context = {
        "department": department,
        "related_courses": related_courses,
        "related_faculty": related_faculty,
        "course_count": related_courses.count(),
        "faculty_count": related_faculty.count(),
    }

    return render(request, "departments/department_profile.html", context)

</div>

    <div class="col-md-4">
        <input type="text" name="q" class="form-control" placeholder="Search Department" value="{{ request.GET.q }}">
    </div>
    <div class="col-auto">
        <button type="submit" class="btn btn-dark">Search</button>
    </div>

        <tr>
            <th>ID</th>
            <th>Name</th>
            <th>Code</th>
            <th>Actions</th>
        </tr>
    </thead>
    <tbody>
        {% for department in departments %}
        <tr>
            <td>{{ department.id }}</td>
            <td>{{ department.name }}</td>
            <td>{{ department.code }}</td>
            <td>
                <a href="/departments/profile/{{ department.id }}/" class="btn btn-info btn-sm">View</a>
                <a href="/departments/edit/{{ department.id }}/" class="btn btn-warning btn-sm">Edit</a>
                <a href="/departments/delete/{{ department.id }}/" class="btn btn-danger btn-sm">Delete</a>
            </td>
        </tr>
        {% empty %}
        <tr>
            <td colspan="4" class="text-center">No departments found.</td>
        </tr>
        {% endfor %}
    </tbody>
</table>

<nav>
    <ul class="pagination">
        {% if departments.has_previous %}
            <li class="page-item">
                <a class="page-link" href="?page=1{% if request.GET.q %}&q={{ request.GET.q }}{% endif %}">First</a>
            </li>
            <li class="page-item">
                <a class="page-link" href="?page={{ departments.previous_page_number }}{% if request.GET.q %}&q={{ request.GET.q }}{% endif %}">Previous</a>
            </li>
        {% endif %}

        <li class="page-item disabled">
            <span class="page-link">Page {{ departments.number }} of {{ departments.paginator.num_pages }}</span>
        </li>

        {% if departments.has_next %}
            <li class="page-item">
                <a class="page-link" href="?page={{ departments.next_page_number }}{% if request.GET.q %}&q={{ request.GET.q }}{% endif %}">Next</a>
            </li>
            <li class="page-item">
                <a class="page-link" href="?page={{ departments.paginator.num_pages }}{% if request.GET.q %}&q={{ request.GET.q }}{% endif %}">Last</a>
            </li>
        {% endif %}
    </ul>
</nav>

1) Department profile + search + pagination
departments/views.py
Isko final version me aisa rakho:

from django.shortcuts import render, redirect, get_object_or_404
from django.contrib import messages
from django.core.paginator import Paginator
from django.db.models import Q
from .models import Department
from .forms import DepartmentForm
from courses.models import Course
from faculty.models import Faculty


def department_list(request):
    query = request.GET.get("q")

    if query:
        department_qs = Department.objects.filter(
            Q(name__icontains=query) | Q(code__icontains=query)
        ).order_by("id")
    else:
        department_qs = Department.objects.all().order_by("id")

    paginator = Paginator(department_qs, 5)
    page_number = request.GET.get("page")
    departments = paginator.get_page(page_number)

    return render(request, "departments/department_list.html", {"departments": departments})


def add_department(request):
    if request.method == "POST":
        form = DepartmentForm(request.POST)
        if form.is_valid():
            form.save()
            messages.success(request, "Department added successfully.")
            return redirect("department_list")
    else:
        form = DepartmentForm()

    return render(request, "departments/add_department.html", {"form": form})


def edit_department(request, pk):
    department = get_object_or_404(Department, pk=pk)

    if request.method == "POST":
        form = DepartmentForm(request.POST, instance=department)
        if form.is_valid():
            form.save()
            messages.success(request, "Department updated successfully.")
            return redirect("department_list")
    else:
        form = DepartmentForm(instance=department)

    return render(request, "departments/edit_department.html", {"form": form})


def delete_department(request, pk):
    department = get_object_or_404(Department, pk=pk)

    if request.method == "POST":
        department.delete()
        messages.success(request, "Department deleted successfully.")
        return redirect("department_list")

    return render(request, "departments/delete_department.html", {"department": department})


def department_profile(request, pk):
    department = get_object_or_404(Department, pk=pk)
    related_courses = Course.objects.filter(department=department).order_by("name")
    related_faculty = Faculty.objects.filter(department=department).order_by("name")

    context = {
        "department": department,
        "related_courses": related_courses,
        "related_faculty": related_faculty,
        "course_count": related_courses.count(),
        "faculty_count": related_faculty.count(),
    }

    return render(request, "departments/department_profile.html", context)

2) Subject module me search + pagination
subjects/views.py
from django.shortcuts import render, redirect, get_object_or_404
from django.contrib import messages
from django.core.paginator import Paginator
from django.db.models import Q
from .models import Subject
from .forms import SubjectForm

def subject_list(request):
    query = request.GET.get("q")

    if query:
        subject_qs = Subject.objects.filter(
            Q(name__icontains=query) |
            Q(code__icontains=query) |
            Q(course__name__icontains=query) |
            Q(faculty__name__icontains=query)
        ).order_by("id")
    else:
        subject_qs = Subject.objects.all().order_by("id")

    paginator = Paginator(subject_qs, 5)
    page_number = request.GET.get("page")
    subjects = paginator.get_page(page_number)

    return render(request, "subjects/subject_list.html", {"subjects": subjects})
templates/subjects/subject_list.html
{% extends "base.html" %}

{% block content %}

{% if messages %}
    {% for message in messages %}
        <div class="alert alert-success">{{ message }}</div>
    {% endfor %}
{% endif %}

<div class="d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center mb-3">
    <h2 class="mb-0">Subject List</h2>
    <a href="/subjects/add/" class="btn btn-primary">Add Subject</a>
</div>

<form method="GET" class="row g-2 mb-3">
    <div class="col-md-4">
        <input type="text" name="q" class="form-control" placeholder="Search Subject" value="{{ request.GET.q }}">
    </div>
    <div class="col-auto">
        <button type="submit" class="btn btn-dark">Search</button>
    </div>
</form>

<div class="table-responsive">
    <table class="table table-bordered table-striped align-middle">
        <thead class="table-dark">
            <tr>
                <th>ID</th>
                <th>Name</th>
                <th>Code</th>
                <th>Course</th>
                <th>Faculty</th>
                <th>Actions</th>
            </tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
        {% for subject in subjects %}
            <tr>
                <td>{{ subject.id }}</td>
                <td>{{ subject.name }}</td>
                <td>{{ subject.code }}</td>
                <td>{{ subject.course }}</td>
                <td>{{ subject.faculty }}</td>
                <td>
                    <a href="/subjects/profile/{{ subject.id }}/" class="btn btn-info btn-sm">View</a>
                    <a href="/subjects/edit/{{ subject.id }}/" class="btn btn-warning btn-sm">Edit</a>
                    <a href="/subjects/delete/{{ subject.id }}/" class="btn btn-danger btn-sm">Delete</a>
                </td>
            </tr>
        {% empty %}
            <tr>
                <td colspan="6" class="text-center">No subjects found.</td>
            </tr>
        {% endfor %}
        </tbody>
    </table>
</div>

<nav>
    <ul class="pagination">
        {% if subjects.has_previous %}
            <li class="page-item"><a class="page-link" href="?page=1{% if request.GET.q %}&q={{ request.GET.q }}{% endif %}">First</a></li>
            <li class="page-item"><a class="page-link" href="?page={{ subjects.previous_page_number }}{% if request.GET.q %}&q={{ request.GET.q }}{% endif %}">Previous</a></li>
        {% endif %}

        <li class="page-item disabled">
            <span class="page-link">Page {{ subjects.number }} of {{ subjects.paginator.num_pages }}</span>
        </li>

        {% if subjects.has_next %}
            <li class="page-item"><a class="page-link" href="?page={{ subjects.next_page_number }}{% if request.GET.q %}&q={{ request.GET.q }}{% endif %}">Next</a></li>
            <li class="page-item"><a class="page-link" href="?page={{ subjects.paginator.num_pages }}{% if request.GET.q %}&q={{ request.GET.q }}{% endif %}">Last</a></li>
        {% endif %}
    </ul>
</nav>

{% endblock %}
3) Course profile me subject/faculty summary + navigation
courses/views.py me course_profile
from subjects.models import Subject

def course_profile(request, pk):
    course = get_object_or_404(Course, pk=pk)
    related_subjects = Subject.objects.filter(course=course).order_by("name")

    return render(request, "courses/course_profile.html", {
        "course": course,
        "related_subjects": related_subjects,
    })

from django.shortcuts import render
from students.models import Student
from faculty.models import Faculty
from courses.models import Course
from subjects.models import Subject
from attendance.models import Attendance
from departments.models import Department

def dashboard(request):
    context = {
        "student_count": Student.objects.count(),
        "faculty_count": Faculty.objects.count(),
        "course_count": Course.objects.count(),
        "subject_count": Subject.objects.count(),
        "attendance_count": Attendance.objects.count(),
        "department_count": Department.objects.count(),
    }
    return render(request, "core/dashboard.html", context)

my core/views.py aisa h        from django.contrib.auth.decorators import login_required
from django.http import HttpResponse
from django.shortcuts import render, redirect, get_object_or_404

from students.models import Student
from .forms import StudentForm

from django.shortcuts import render
from students.models import Student
from faculty.models import Faculty
from courses.models import Course
from subjects.models import Subject
from attendance.models import Attendance
from departments.models import Department



@login_required
def dashboard(request):

    if request.user.is_superuser:
        return HttpResponse("Admin Dashboard")

    elif hasattr(request.user, 'role') and request.user.role == 'student':
        return HttpResponse("Student Dashboard")

    elif hasattr(request.user, 'role') and request.user.role == 'faculty':
        return HttpResponse("Faculty Dashboard")

    else:
        return HttpResponse("Logged in, but no role assigned.")
    
    context = {
        "student_count": Student.objects.count(),
        "faculty_count": Faculty.objects.count(),
        "course_count": Course.objects.count(),
        "subject_count": Subject.objects.count(),
        "attendance_count": Attendance.objects.count(),
        "department_count": Department.objects.count(),
    }


@login_required
def student_list(request):
    students = Student.objects.all()
    return render(request, "core/student_list.html", {"students": students})


@login_required
def add_student(request):
    if request.method == "POST":
        form = StudentForm(request.POST)
        if form.is_valid():
            form.save()
            return redirect("student_list")
    else:
        form = StudentForm()
    return render(request, "core/student_form.html", {"form": form})


@login_required
def edit_student(request, pk):
    student = get_object_or_404(Student, pk=pk)

    if request.method == "POST":
        form = StudentForm(request.POST, instance=student)
        if form.is_valid():
            form.save()
            return redirect("student_list")
    else:
        form = StudentForm(instance=student)

    return render(request, "core/student_form.html", {"form": form})


@login_required
def delete_student(request, pk):
    student = get_object_or_404(Student, pk=pk)
    student.delete()
    return redirect("student_list")    or mere core templates  m koi dashboard.html nhi h 

Samajh gaya. Tumhare dashboard() function me problem ye hai ki context kabhi use hi nahi ho raha, kyunki usse pehle hi return HttpResponse(...) ho ja raha hai.

Isliye chahe tum dashboard.html banao ya na banao, abhi wo render nahi hoga.

Tumhare current code me exact issue
Ye part:

if request.user.is_superuser:
    return HttpResponse("Admin Dashboard")
jaise hi chalega, function wahi khatam ho jayega.

Aur ye neeche wala part:

context = {
    "student_count": Student.objects.count(),
    ...
}
kabhi execute hi nahi hoga.

Best fix
Agar tum ab proper Bootstrap dashboard banana chahte ho, to core/views.py me dashboard() ko is version se replace karo:

from django.contrib.auth.decorators import login_required
from django.shortcuts import render, redirect, get_object_or_404

from students.models import Student
from .forms import StudentForm
from faculty.models import Faculty
from courses.models import Course
from subjects.models import Subject
from attendance.models import Attendance
from departments.models import Department


@login_required
def dashboard(request):
    context = {
        "student_count": Student.objects.count(),
        "faculty_count": Faculty.objects.count(),
        "course_count": Course.objects.count(),
        "subject_count": Subject.objects.count(),
        "attendance_count": Attendance.objects.count(),
        "department_count": Department.objects.count(),
        "user_role": "Admin" if request.user.is_superuser else getattr(request.user, "role", "No Role"),
    }

    return render(request, "core/dashboard.html", context)


@login_required
def student_list(request):
    students = Student.objects.all()
    return render(request, "core/student_list.html", {"students": students})


@login_required
def add_student(request):
    if request.method == "POST":
        form = StudentForm(request.POST)
        if form.is_valid():
            form.save()
            return redirect("student_list")
    else:
        form = StudentForm()
    return render(request, "core/student_form.html", {"form": form})


@login_required
def edit_student(request, pk):
    student = get_object_or_404(Student, pk=pk)

    if request.method == "POST":
        form = StudentForm(request.POST, instance=student)
        if form.is_valid():
            form.save()
            return redirect("student_list")
    else:
        form = StudentForm(instance=student)

    return render(request, "core/student_form.html", {"form": form})


@login_required
def delete_student(request, pk):
    student = get_object_or_404(Student, pk=pk)
    student.delete()
    return redirect("student_list")

File me khud bhi ye breakdown diya gaya hai, aur proposed stack Django + SQLite + HTML/CSS/Bootstrap/JS rakha gaya tha.

gpt data

Ab seedha useful analysis:

Video me kya lag raha hai
Ye ek demo-first ERP / management system showcase type project hai, jahan aim complete polished college/school admin workflow dikhana hai, na ki necessarily production-grade architecture. Is type ke systems me generally ye modules hote hain: login/logout, admin/staff/student roles, student CRUD, faculty/staff management, course/department assignment, attendance, exams/results, fee records, aur dashboard counts/charts. Tumhari file me bhi exactly ye module list compare ki gayi hai. 
gpt data

 
gpt data

accounts, students, faculty, courses, attendance, exams, finance, core, config

Ye architecture file me “very good / professional Django split” bola gaya hai.

gpt data

Video/demo type ERP me dashboard visually important hota hai. Tumhare yahan अभी counts/charts/summary cards/proper panel polish missing side me hain. File me dashboard analytics target me total students, total teachers, graphs mention hai. 
gpt data


7. Role-based user experience incomplete
Login hai, lekin admin/faculty/student ke liye clearly separate dashboards, permissions, route restrictions aur menu visibility abhi polish maangti hai. File me authentication aur role-based dashboards core goal the. 
gpt data

  File "C:\Users\lenovo\college_erp\venv\Lib\site-packages\django\core\handlers\base.py", line 333, in check_response

[07/Mar/2026 13:29:59] "GET /dashboard/ HTTP/1.1" 500 70719  

Error ka exact reason tumhare dashboard view me hai.

Tumhare code me ye structure tha:

@login_required
def dashboard(request):

    if request.user.is_superuser:
        return HttpResponse("Admin Dashboard")

    elif hasattr(request.user, 'role') and request.user.role == 'student':
        return HttpResponse("Student Dashboard")

    elif hasattr(request.user, 'role') and request.user.role == 'faculty':
        return HttpResponse("Faculty Dashboard")

    else:
        return HttpResponse("Logged in, but no role assigned.")
    
    context = {
        "student_count": Student.objects.count(),
        "faculty_count": Faculty.objects.count(),
        "course_count": Course.objects.count(),
        "subject_count": Subject.objects.count(),
        "attendance_count": Attendance.objects.count(),
        "department_count": Department.objects.count(),
    }
Problem kya hai
context = {...} ke neeche koi return nahi hai.

Aur ek aur baat:
upar jitne bhi return HttpResponse(...) lage hue hain, unke baad function wahi khatam ho jaata hai.
Iska matlab context block kabhi execute hi nahi hota.

Isliye Django bol raha hai:

The view core.views.dashboard didn't return an HttpResponse object. It returned None instead.

Ye tab hota hai jab function ke end tak pahunch jaaye aur kuch return na kare.

Sahi fix
Agar tum dashboard page render karna chahte ho, to HttpResponse(...) ki jagah template render karo.

Correct version
core/views.py

from django.contrib.auth.decorators import login_required
from django.shortcuts import render
from students.models import Student
from faculty.models import Faculty
from courses.models import Course
from subjects.models import Subject
from attendance.models import Attendance
from departments.models import Department


@login_required
def dashboard(request):
    context = {
        "student_count": Student.objects.count(),
        "faculty_count": Faculty.objects.count(),
        "course_count": Course.objects.count(),
        "subject_count": Subject.objects.count(),
        "attendance_count": Attendance.objects.count(),
        "department_count": Department.objects.count(),
    }

    if request.user.is_superuser:
        context["user_type"] = "Admin"

    elif hasattr(request.user, 'role') and request.user.role == 'student':
        context["user_type"] = "Student"

    elif hasattr(request.user, 'role') and request.user.role == 'faculty':
        context["user_type"] = "Faculty"

    else:
        context["user_type"] = "No Role Assigned"

    return render(request, "core/dashboard.html", context)
Dashboard template banao
templates/core/dashboard.html

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Dashboard</title>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>{{ user_type }} Dashboard</h1>

    <p>Total Students: {{ student_count }}</p>
    <p>Total Faculty: {{ faculty_count }}</p>
    <p>Total Courses: {{ course_count }}</p>
    <p>Total Subjects: {{ subject_count }}</p>
    <p>Total Attendance Records: {{ attendance_count }}</p>
    <p>Total Departments: {{ department_count }}</p>
</body>
</html>
Agar sirf test karna hai abhi
Agar abhi template nahi banana aur bas error hatana hai, to temporarily ye likh do:

@login_required
def dashboard(request):
    return HttpResponse("Dashboard working")
Lekin ye sirf testing ke liye hai. Final project ke liye render() use karna better hai.

Tumhare current code me logical mistake
Tumne pehle hi return kar diya:

if ...
    return ...
elif ...
    return ...
elif ...
    return ...
else:
    return ...
Uske baad ye part:

context = {...}
kabhi chalega hi nahi.

Matlab current function ka real flow aisa hai:

ya to early return

ya end me None

Isliye clean approach ye hai:

pehle context banao

user role check karo

last me ek hi return render(...)

Best final version for your ERP
Main tumhe thoda better professional version de raha hoon:

from django.contrib.auth.decorators import login_required
from django.shortcuts import render
from students.models import Student
from faculty.models import Faculty
from courses.models import Course
from subjects.models import Subject
from attendance.models import Attendance
from departments.models import Department


@login_required
def dashboard(request):
    context = {
        "student_count": Student.objects.count(),
        "faculty_count": Faculty.objects.count(),
        "course_count": Course.objects.count(),
        "subject_count": Subject.objects.count(),
        "attendance_count": Attendance.objects.count(),
        "department_count": Department.objects.count(),
    }

    if request.user.is_superuser:
        context["dashboard_title"] = "Admin Dashboard"
    elif getattr(request.user, "role", None) == "student":
        context["dashboard_title"] = "Student Dashboard"
    elif getattr(request.user, "role", None) == "faculty":
        context["dashboard_title"] = "Faculty Dashboard"
    else:
        context["dashboard_title"] = "Dashboard"

    return render(request, "core/dashboard.html", context)
Template:

<h1>{{ dashboard_title }}</h1>
<p>Total Students: {{ student_count }}</p>
<p>Total Faculty: {{ faculty_count }}</p>
<p>Total Courses: {{ course_count }}</p>
<p>Total Subjects: {{ subject_count }}</p>
<p>Total Attendance: {{ attendance_count }}</p>
<p>Total Departments: {{ department_count }}</p>
Ab tum kya karo
core/views.py me above wala fixed code paste karo

templates/core/dashboard.html banao

server restart karo

/dashboard/ open karo

Agar phir bhi error aaye, next issue most probably hoga:

template not found

model import path issue

role field custom user me missing

Tum core/views.py ka updated code bhejo, main usko line by line correct karke final clean version de dunga.

Exam / Result System ka Structure
Hum 3 cheezein banayenge:

Example:

Student     Subject      Marks

Example:

Student: Rahul
Python: 85
Java: 78
Total: 163
Percentage: 81%

Step 1 → Exam Model
Agar exams app hai to:

exams/models.py

from django.db import models
from courses.models import Course


class Exam(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    course = models.ForeignKey(Course, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    date = models.DateField()

    def __str__(self):
        return self.name
Step 2 → Result Model
Student + Subject + Exam + Marks
exams/models.py

from students.models import Student
from subjects.models import Subject


class Result(models.Model):
    student = models.ForeignKey(Student, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    subject = models.ForeignKey(Subject, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    exam = models.ForeignKey(Exam, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    marks = models.IntegerField()

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.student} - {self.subject} - {self.marks}"
Step 3 → Migration
Run:

python manage.py makemigrations
python manage.py migrate
Step 4 → Admin Panel
exams/admin.py

from django.contrib import admin
from .models import Exam, Result

admin.site.register(Exam)
admin.site.register(Result)
Ab admin panel me:

Exam add
Result add
dikhne lagega.

(venv) PS C:\Users\lenovo\college_erp> python manage.py makemigrations 
>> python manage.py migrate
Was result.marks_obtained renamed to result.marks (a IntegerField)? [y/N]

Django tumse migration confirmation maang raha hai.

Message ka matlab:

marks_obtained field ko rename karke marks kiya gaya hai kya?

Agar tumne models.py me ye change kiya hai:
Old

marks_obtained = models.IntegerField()
New

marks = models.IntegerField()
To Django pooch raha hai ki:

kya ye rename hai ya naya field hai

Kya press karna chahiye
✅ y press karo

y
Phir enter press karo.

Isse Django samjhega:

marks_obtained → marks
Aur database me data delete nahi hoga, sirf field rename hogi.

Agar N press karte
Agar N karte to Django:

marks_obtained delete karta

marks new field create karta

purana data lose ho sakta tha

Isliye y correct option hai.

Next kya hoga
Migration complete hone ke baad:

Applying exams.000X_auto_...
OK
Uske baad check karo
Run:

python manage.py runserver
Phir admin panel open karo:

http://127.0.0.1:8000/admin

Results

Ye Django ka normal migration question hai. Panic karne ki zarurat nahi 👍

Error ka matlab
Tumne Exam model me new field name add ki hai, aur database me already exam table exist karta hai.

Database ke existing rows me name empty hai, isliye Django pooch raha hai:

un purane rows me kya value daalu?

Correct Option
👉 Option 1 select karo

Type karo:

1
Enter press karo.

Phir Django poochega default value
Example:

Please enter the default value now:
Tum yeh likh sakte ho:

"Exam"
ya

"Default Exam"
Example:

Default Exam
Enter.

Phir run hoga
Migrations for 'exams'
Applying exams.000X_auto...
OK
Simple Samjho
Database me jo purane exam records hain, unke liye Django automatically:

name = "Default Exam"
set kar dega.

ValueError: Field 'id' expected a number but got 'Default Exam'.       
(venv) PS C:\Users\lenovo\college_erp> 

Issue mil gaya.

Tumne galti se Result.subject ke liye default me:

'Default Exam'
de diya, jabki subject ek ForeignKey hai.
ForeignKey ko string nahi, us related table ka numeric id chahiye hota hai.

Isliye error aaya:

Field 'id' expected a number but got 'Default Exam'

Ab fix ka sahi tarika
Isko clean tarike se thik karte hain.

Step 1: failed migration file delete karo
Folder kholo:

exams/migrations/
Wahan jo nayi migration bani hai, usko delete karo:

0002_rename_marks_obtained_result_marks_and_more.py
__init__.py ko mat delete karna.

Step 2: models ko thoda safe banao
Abhi existing DB me old data hai, isliye naye required fields directly add karne par problem aa rahi hai.

exams/models.py me temporarily subject ko nullable kar do:

subject = models.ForeignKey(Subject, on_delete=models.CASCADE, null=True, blank=True)
Aur name field ko bhi temporary default de do:

name = models.CharField(max_length=100, default="Exam")
Example
from django.db import models
from courses.models import Course
from students.models import Student
from subjects.models import Subject


class Exam(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, default="Exam")
    course = models.ForeignKey(Course, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    date = models.DateField()

    def __str__(self):
        return self.name


class Result(models.Model):
    student = models.ForeignKey(Student, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    subject = models.ForeignKey(Subject, on_delete=models.CASCADE, null=True, blank=True)
    exam = models.ForeignKey(Exam, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    marks = models.IntegerField()

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.student} - {self.subject} - {self.marks}"
Step 3: phir migrations chalao
python manage.py makemigrations
python manage.py migrate
Ab is baar prompt ya to nahi aayega, ya manageable hoga.

marks_obtained renamed to marks? → y

baaki prompts ideally nahi aane chahiye

Ye kyun kaam karega
Kyuki:

name ke liye default "Exam" mil gaya

subject nullable ho gaya, to old rows me blank reh sakta hai

Django ko fake string ko FK id me convert nahi karna padega

Agar tum old exam/result data ki tension nahi le rahe
Agar ye sab test data tha aur delete ho sakta hai, to aur bhi easy fix hai:

Easy reset option
exams/migrations/0002... delete karo

database me exams/result old records delete karo

phir migration chalao

Lekin safest option wahi hai jo maine upar diya.

Important
Abhi subject ko temporary nullable rakho.
Baad me jab result add form ban jayega aur saare old rows clean ho jayenge, tab chahe to ise non-nullable kar denge.

Next ab kya karo

Step 3: exams/models.py ko safe version me rakho
Ye code rakho:

from django.db import models
from courses.models import Course
from students.models import Student
from subjects.models import Subject


class Exam(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, default="Exam")
    course = models.ForeignKey(Course, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    date = models.DateField()

    def __str__(self):
        return self.name


class Result(models.Model):
    student = models.ForeignKey(Student, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    subject = models.ForeignKey(Subject, on_delete=models.CASCADE, null=True, blank=True)
    exam = models.ForeignKey(Exam, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    marks = models.IntegerField()

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.student} - {self.subject} - {self.marks}"

Step 1: exams/forms.py
Ye file banao:

from django import forms
from .models import Exam, Result


class ExamForm(forms.ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = Exam
        fields = ['name', 'course', 'date']
        widgets = {
            'date': forms.DateInput(attrs={'type': 'date', 'class': 'form-control'}),
            'name': forms.TextInput(attrs={'class': 'form-control'}),
            'course': forms.Select(attrs={'class': 'form-select'}),
        }


class ResultForm(forms.ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = Result
        fields = ['student', 'subject', 'exam', 'marks']
        widgets = {
            'student': forms.Select(attrs={'class': 'form-select'}),
            'subject': forms.Select(attrs={'class': 'form-select'}),
            'exam': forms.Select(attrs={'class': 'form-select'}),
            'marks': forms.NumberInput(attrs={'class': 'form-control'}),
        }
Step 2: exams/views.py
Isme ye views likho:

from django.contrib import messages
from django.shortcuts import render, redirect, get_object_or_404
from .models import Exam, Result
from .forms import ExamForm, ResultForm


def exam_add(request):
    form = ExamForm(request.POST or None)
    if form.is_valid():
        form.save()
        messages.success(request, "Exam added successfully.")
        return redirect('exam_list')
    return render(request, 'exams/exam_form.html', {'form': form})


def exam_list(request):
    exams = Exam.objects.all().order_by('-date')
    return render(request, 'exams/exam_list.html', {'exams': exams})


def result_add(request):
    form = ResultForm(request.POST or None)
    if form.is_valid():
        form.save()
        messages.success(request, "Result added successfully.")
        return redirect('result_list')
    return render(request, 'exams/result_form.html', {'form': form})


def result_list(request):
    results = Result.objects.select_related('student', 'subject', 'exam').all()
    return render(request, 'exams/result_list.html', {'results': results})


def student_result_card(request, student_id):
    results = Result.objects.select_related('student', 'subject', 'exam').filter(student_id=student_id)

    student = None
    total_marks = 0
    count = results.count()

    if count > 0:
        student = results.first().student
        total_marks = sum(r.marks for r in results)
        percentage = total_marks / count
    else:
        percentage = 0

    if percentage >= 90:
        grade = 'A+'
    elif percentage >= 80:
        grade = 'A'
    elif percentage >= 70:
        grade = 'B'
    elif percentage >= 60:
        grade = 'C'
    elif percentage >= 50:
        grade = 'D'
    else:
        grade = 'F'

    context = {
        'student': student,
        'results': results,
        'total_marks': total_marks,
        'percentage': percentage,
        'grade': grade,
    }
    return render(request, 'exams/student_result_card.html', context)
Step 3: exams/urls.py
Agar file nahi hai to banao:

from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path('add/', views.exam_add, name='exam_add'),
    path('list/', views.exam_list, name='exam_list'),
    path('results/add/', views.result_add, name='result_add'),
    path('results/list/', views.result_list, name='result_list'),
    path('student/<int:student_id>/', views.student_result_card, name='student_result_card'),
]
Step 4: project main urls me include karo

path('exams/', include('exams.urls')),
Aur top par import ho:

from django.urls import path, include

        <th>Marks</th>
    </tr>

    <tr>
        <td>{{ result.student }}</td>
        <td>{{ result.subject }}</td>
        <td>{{ result.exam }}</td>
        <td>{{ result.marks }}</td>
    </tr>
    {% endfor %}
</table>
templates/exams/student_result_card.html
<h2>Student Result Card</h2>

{% if student %}
    <h3>{{ student }}</h3>

    <table border="1">
        <tr>
            <th>Subject</th>
            <th>Exam</th>
            <th>Marks</th>
        </tr>
        {% for result in results %}
        <tr>
            <td>{{ result.subject }}</td>
            <td>{{ result.exam.name }}</td>
            <td>{{ result.marks }}</td>
        </tr>
        {% endfor %}
    </table>

    <p><strong>Total Marks:</strong> {{ total_marks }}</p>
    <p><strong>Percentage:</strong> {{ percentage }}%</p>
    <p><strong>Grade:</strong> {{ grade }}</p>
{% else %}
    <p>No result found for this student.</p>
{% endif %}

Bootstrap styled pages

pass/fail status

duplicate result entry rokna

Sabse pehle ek important improvement
Abhi student_result_card me percentage simple average se aa raha hai:

percentage = total_marks / count
Ye technically average marks hai, percentage tab sahi hoga jab har subject total marks known ho.
Abhi ke liye do options hain:

Option A: easy
Isko Average Marks naam de do

Option B: better
Exam ya Result me total_marks field add karo, phir real percentage nikalein

Abhi practical tareeka:
result card me “Percentage” ki jagah “Average Marks” likh do, jab tak proper total marks logic na bana lo.

Ab next kaam: Result module ko thoda improve karo
1. Duplicate result entry roko
exams/models.py me Result model ke andar ye add karo:

class Meta:
    unique_together = ['student', 'subject', 'exam']
Isse same student + same subject + same exam ka duplicate result save nahi hoga.

2. Better __str__
def __str__(self):
    return f"{self.student} - {self.subject} - {self.exam} - {self.marks}"
3. Result card view improve
views.py me percentage variable ko temporary average_marks bana do:

average_marks = total_marks / count if count > 0 else 0
Aur grade isi par based rakho.

4. Template text improve
student_result_card.html me:

<p><strong>Average Marks:</strong> {{ average_marks }}</p>
percentage hata do ya baad me real percentage ke saath laayenge.

1) exams/models.py
from django.db import models
from courses.models import Course
from students.models import Student
from subjects.models import Subject


class Exam(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, default="Exam")
    course = models.ForeignKey(Course, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    date = models.DateField()

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.name} - {self.course}"


class Result(models.Model):
    student = models.ForeignKey(Student, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    subject = models.ForeignKey(Subject, on_delete=models.CASCADE, null=True, blank=True)
    exam = models.ForeignKey(Exam, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    marks = models.IntegerField()

    class Meta:
        unique_together = ['student', 'subject', 'exam']

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.student} - {self.subject} - {self.exam} - {self.marks}"
2) exams/forms.py
from django import forms
from .models import Exam, Result


class ExamForm(forms.ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = Exam
        fields = ['name', 'course', 'date']
        widgets = {
            'name': forms.TextInput(attrs={
                'class': 'form-control',
                'placeholder': 'Enter exam name'
            }),
            'course': forms.Select(attrs={
                'class': 'form-select'
            }),
            'date': forms.DateInput(attrs={
                'type': 'date',
                'class': 'form-control'
            }),
        }


class ResultForm(forms.ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = Result
        fields = ['student', 'subject', 'exam', 'marks']
        widgets = {
            'student': forms.Select(attrs={
                'class': 'form-select'
            }),
            'subject': forms.Select(attrs={
                'class': 'form-select'
            }),
            'exam': forms.Select(attrs={
                'class': 'form-select'
            }),
            'marks': forms.NumberInput(attrs={
                'class': 'form-control',
                'placeholder': 'Enter marks'
            }),
        }

    def clean_marks(self):
        marks = self.cleaned_data.get('marks')
        if marks is not None and marks < 0:
            raise forms.ValidationError("Marks cannot be negative.")
        return marks
3) exams/views.py
from django.contrib import messages
from django.shortcuts import render, redirect
from .models import Exam, Result
from .forms import ExamForm, ResultForm


def exam_add(request):
    form = ExamForm(request.POST or None)
    if form.is_valid():
        form.save()
        messages.success(request, "Exam added successfully.")
        return redirect('exam_list')

    return render(request, 'exams/exam_form.html', {'form': form})


def exam_list(request):
    exams = Exam.objects.select_related('course').all().order_by('-date')
    return render(request, 'exams/exam_list.html', {'exams': exams})


def result_add(request):
    form = ResultForm(request.POST or None)
    if form.is_valid():
        form.save()
        messages.success(request, "Result added successfully.")
        return redirect('result_list')

    return render(request, 'exams/result_form.html', {'form': form})


def result_list(request):
    results = Result.objects.select_related('student', 'subject', 'exam').all().order_by('student')
    return render(request, 'exams/result_list.html', {'results': results})


def student_result_card(request, student_id):
    results = Result.objects.select_related('student', 'subject', 'exam').filter(student_id=student_id)

    student = None
    total_marks = 0
    count = results.count()
    average_marks = 0
    grade = "F"
    status = "Fail"

    if count > 0:
        student = results.first().student
        total_marks = sum(result.marks for result in results)
        average_marks = total_marks / count

        if average_marks >= 90:
            grade = 'A+'
        elif average_marks >= 80:
            grade = 'A'
        elif average_marks >= 70:
            grade = 'B'
        elif average_marks >= 60:
            grade = 'C'
        elif average_marks >= 50:
            grade = 'D'
        else:
            grade = 'F'

        if average_marks >= 40:
            status = "Pass"
        else:
            status = "Fail"

    context = {
        'student': student,
        'results': results,
        'total_marks': total_marks,
        'average_marks': round(average_marks, 2),
        'grade': grade,
        'status': status,
    }
    return render(request, 'exams/student_result_card.html', context)
4) exams/urls.py
from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path('add/', views.exam_add, name='exam_add'),
    path('list/', views.exam_list, name='exam_list'),
    path('results/add/', views.result_add, name='result_add'),
    path('results/list/', views.result_list, name='result_list'),
    path('student/<int:student_id>/', views.student_result_card, name='student_result_card'),
]

6) exams/admin.py
from django.contrib import admin
from .models import Exam, Result


@admin.register(Exam)
class ExamAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):
    list_display = ['id', 'name', 'course', 'date']
    search_fields = ['name', 'course__name']
    list_filter = ['date']


@admin.register(Result)
class ResultAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):
    list_display = ['id', 'student', 'subject', 'exam', 'marks']
    search_fields = ['student__name', 'subject__name', 'exam__name']
    list_filter = ['exam', 'subject']
Agar student__name ya course__name tumhare model ke actual field names se different hain, to unhe apne model ke hisaab se change kar lena.

Full Finance Module Code
1) finance/models.py
from django.db import models
from students.models import Student


class Fee(models.Model):
    STATUS_CHOICES = [
        ('Paid', 'Paid'),
        ('Pending', 'Pending'),
        ('Partial', 'Partial'),
    ]

    student = models.ForeignKey(Student, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    total_fee = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2)
    paid_amount = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, default=0)
    payment_date = models.DateField()
    status = models.CharField(max_length=10, choices=STATUS_CHOICES, default='Pending')

    def pending_amount(self):
        return self.total_fee - self.paid_amount

    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
        if self.paid_amount >= self.total_fee:
            self.status = 'Paid'
        elif self.paid_amount > 0:
            self.status = 'Partial'
        else:
            self.status = 'Pending'
        super().save(*args, **kwargs)

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.student} - {self.status}"
2) finance/forms.py
from django import forms
from .models import Fee


class FeeForm(forms.ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = Fee
        fields = ['student', 'total_fee', 'paid_amount', 'payment_date']
        widgets = {
            'student': forms.Select(attrs={'class': 'form-select'}),
            'total_fee': forms.NumberInput(attrs={'class': 'form-control', 'placeholder': 'Enter total fee'}),
            'paid_amount': forms.NumberInput(attrs={'class': 'form-control', 'placeholder': 'Enter paid amount'}),
            'payment_date': forms.DateInput(attrs={'type': 'date', 'class': 'form-control'}),
        }

    def clean(self):
        cleaned_data = super().clean()
        total_fee = cleaned_data.get('total_fee')
        paid_amount = cleaned_data.get('paid_amount')

        if total_fee is not None and paid_amount is not None:
            if paid_amount < 0:
                raise forms.ValidationError("Paid amount cannot be negative.")
            if total_fee < 0:
                raise forms.ValidationError("Total fee cannot be negative.")
            if paid_amount > total_fee:
                raise forms.ValidationError("Paid amount cannot be greater than total fee.")

        return cleaned_data
3) finance/views.py
from django.contrib import messages
from django.shortcuts import render, redirect
from .models import Fee
from .forms import FeeForm


def fee_add(request):
    form = FeeForm(request.POST or None)
    if form.is_valid():
        form.save()
        messages.success(request, "Fee record added successfully.")
        return redirect('fee_list')
    return render(request, 'finance/fee_form.html', {'form': form})


def fee_list(request):
    fees = Fee.objects.select_related('student').all().order_by('-payment_date')
    return render(request, 'finance/fee_list.html', {'fees': fees})


def student_fee_history(request, student_id):
    fees = Fee.objects.select_related('student').filter(student_id=student_id).order_by('-payment_date')

    student = None
    total_fee = 0
    total_paid = 0
    total_pending = 0

    if fees.exists():
        student = fees.first().student
        total_fee = sum(fee.total_fee for fee in fees)
        total_paid = sum(fee.paid_amount for fee in fees)
        total_pending = total_fee - total_paid

    context = {
        'student': student,
        'fees': fees,
        'total_fee': total_fee,
        'total_paid': total_paid,
        'total_pending': total_pending,
    }
    return render(request, 'finance/student_fee_history.html', context)
4) finance/urls.py
from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path('add/', views.fee_add, name='fee_add'),
    path('list/', views.fee_list, name='fee_list'),
    path('student/<int:student_id>/', views.student_fee_history, name='student_fee_history'),
]

    list_filter = ['status', 'payment_date']
    search_fields = ['student__name']

            </table>

            <div class="mt-4">
                <p><strong>Total Fee:</strong> {{ total_fee }}</p>
                <p><strong>Total Paid:</strong> {{ total_paid }}</p>
                <p><strong>Total Pending:</strong> {{ total_pending }}</p>
            </div>
        {% else %}
            <div class="alert alert-warning">
                No fee history found for this student.
            </div>
        {% endif %}
    </div>
</div>
</body>
</html>
10) migration run karo
python manage.py makemigrations
python manage.py migrate

12) ek important note
Agar finance/models.py me pehle se old Fee model hai, to migration prompts aa sakte hain.
Aise case me prompt ka screenshot/text bhej dena, main exact batata hoon kya select karna hai.

Iske baad tumhare project me major missing modules me se Fees bhi done ho jayega.


ye exam module fully complete ho gya...?

Main clearly bata deta hoon kya complete hai aur kya polish baaki hai.

1) exams/models.py
from django.db import models
from courses.models import Course
from students.models import Student
from subjects.models import Subject


class Exam(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, default="Exam")
    course = models.ForeignKey(Course, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    date = models.DateField()

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.name} - {self.course}"


class Result(models.Model):
    student = models.ForeignKey(Student, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    subject = models.ForeignKey(Subject, on_delete=models.CASCADE, null=True, blank=True)
    exam = models.ForeignKey(Exam, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    marks = models.PositiveIntegerField()

    class Meta:
        unique_together = ['student', 'subject', 'exam']
        ordering = ['student']

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.student} - {self.subject} - {self.exam} - {self.marks}"
2) exams/forms.py
from django import forms
from .models import Exam, Result


class ExamForm(forms.ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = Exam
        fields = ['name', 'course', 'date']
        widgets = {
            'name': forms.TextInput(attrs={
                'class': 'form-control',
                'placeholder': 'Enter exam name'
            }),
            'course': forms.Select(attrs={
                'class': 'form-select'
            }),
            'date': forms.DateInput(attrs={
                'type': 'date',
                'class': 'form-control'
            }),
        }


class ResultForm(forms.ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = Result
        fields = ['student', 'subject', 'exam', 'marks']
        widgets = {
            'student': forms.Select(attrs={'class': 'form-select'}),
            'subject': forms.Select(attrs={'class': 'form-select'}),
            'exam': forms.Select(attrs={'class': 'form-select'}),
            'marks': forms.NumberInput(attrs={
                'class': 'form-control',
                'placeholder': 'Enter marks'
            }),
        }

    def clean_marks(self):
        marks = self.cleaned_data.get('marks')
        if marks is None:
            return marks
        if marks < 0:
            raise forms.ValidationError("Marks cannot be negative.")
        if marks > 100:
            raise forms.ValidationError("Marks cannot be greater than 100.")
        return marks
3) exams/views.py
from django.contrib import messages
from django.db.models import Q
from django.shortcuts import render, redirect, get_object_or_404
from .models import Exam, Result
from .forms import ExamForm, ResultForm


# =========================
# Exam CRUD
# =========================

def exam_add(request):
    form = ExamForm(request.POST or None)
    if form.is_valid():
        form.save()
        messages.success(request, "Exam added successfully.")
        return redirect('exam_list')
    return render(request, 'exams/exam_form.html', {'form': form, 'title': 'Add Exam'})


def exam_list(request):
    exams = Exam.objects.select_related('course').all().order_by('-date')
    return render(request, 'exams/exam_list.html', {'exams': exams})


def exam_edit(request, pk):
    exam = get_object_or_404(Exam, pk=pk)
    form = ExamForm(request.POST or None, instance=exam)
    if form.is_valid():
        form.save()
        messages.success(request, "Exam updated successfully.")
        return redirect('exam_list')
    return render(request, 'exams/exam_form.html', {'form': form, 'title': 'Edit Exam'})


def exam_delete(request, pk):
    exam = get_object_or_404(Exam, pk=pk)
    if request.method == 'POST':
        exam.delete()
        messages.success(request, "Exam deleted successfully.")
        return redirect('exam_list')
    return render(request, 'exams/exam_confirm_delete.html', {'exam': exam})


# =========================
# Result CRUD
# =========================

def result_add(request):
    form = ResultForm(request.POST or None)
    if form.is_valid():
        form.save()
        messages.success(request, "Result added successfully.")
        return redirect('result_list')
    return render(request, 'exams/result_form.html', {'form': form, 'title': 'Add Result'})


def result_list(request):
    query = request.GET.get('q', '')
    exam_id = request.GET.get('exam', '')
    subject_id = request.GET.get('subject', '')

    results = Result.objects.select_related('student', 'subject', 'exam').all()

    if query:
        results = results.filter(
            Q(student__name__icontains=query) |
            Q(subject__name__icontains=query) |
            Q(exam__name__icontains=query)
        )

    if exam_id:
        results = results.filter(exam_id=exam_id)

    if subject_id:
        results = results.filter(subject_id=subject_id)

    exams = Exam.objects.all()
    subject_ids = Result.objects.values_list('subject_id', flat=True).distinct()
    from subjects.models import Subject
    subjects = Subject.objects.filter(id__in=subject_ids)

    context = {
        'results': results,
        'exams': exams,
        'subjects': subjects,
        'query': query,
        'selected_exam': exam_id,
        'selected_subject': subject_id,
    }
    return render(request, 'exams/result_list.html', context)


def result_edit(request, pk):
    result = get_object_or_404(Result, pk=pk)
    form = ResultForm(request.POST or None, instance=result)
    if form.is_valid():
        form.save()
        messages.success(request, "Result updated successfully.")
        return redirect('result_list')
    return render(request, 'exams/result_form.html', {'form': form, 'title': 'Edit Result'})


def result_delete(request, pk):
    result = get_object_or_404(Result, pk=pk)
    if request.method == 'POST':
        result.delete()
        messages.success(request, "Result deleted successfully.")
        return redirect('result_list')
    return render(request, 'exams/result_confirm_delete.html', {'result': result})


# =========================
# Student Result Card
# =========================

def student_result_card(request, student_id):
    results = Result.objects.select_related('student', 'subject', 'exam').filter(student_id=student_id)

    student = None
    total_marks = 0
    count = results.count()
    average_marks = 0
    grade = "F"
    status = "Fail"

    if count > 0:
        student = results.first().student
        total_marks = sum(result.marks for result in results)
        average_marks = total_marks / count

        if average_marks >= 90:
            grade = 'A+'
        elif average_marks >= 80:
            grade = 'A'
        elif average_marks >= 70:
            grade = 'B'
        elif average_marks >= 60:
            grade = 'C'
        elif average_marks >= 50:
            grade = 'D'
        else:
            grade = 'F'

        if average_marks >= 40:
            status = "Pass"
        else:
            status = "Fail"

    context = {
        'student': student,
        'results': results,
        'total_marks': total_marks,
        'average_marks': round(average_marks, 2),
        'grade': grade,
        'status': status,
    }
    return render(request, 'exams/student_result_card.html', context)
4) exams/urls.py
from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path('add/', views.exam_add, name='exam_add'),
    path('list/', views.exam_list, name='exam_list'),
    path('edit/<int:pk>/', views.exam_edit, name='exam_edit'),
    path('delete/<int:pk>/', views.exam_delete, name='exam_delete'),

    path('results/add/', views.result_add, name='result_add'),
    path('results/list/', views.result_list, name='result_list'),
    path('results/edit/<int:pk>/', views.result_edit, name='result_edit'),
    path('results/delete/<int:pk>/', views.result_delete, name='result_delete'),

    path('student/<int:student_id>/', views.student_result_card, name='student_result_card'),
]
5) exams/admin.py
from django.contrib import admin
from .models import Exam, Result


@admin.register(Exam)
class ExamAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):
    list_display = ['id', 'name', 'course', 'date']
    search_fields = ['name', 'course__name']
    list_filter = ['date']


@admin.register(Result)
class ResultAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):
    list_display = ['id', 'student', 'subject', 'exam', 'marks']
    search_fields = ['student__name', 'subject__name', 'exam__name']
    list_filter = ['exam', 'subject']
Agar student__name ya course__name tumhare actual field names se different hai, to usko apne model ke hisaab se change kar dena.

ho gya run kya ab hmara exam module complete h ya kuch reh gya h 

Short answer: haan, tumhara Exam module practically complete ho gaya hai.
Jo features ek college ERP exam module me hone chahiye, wo almost sab implement ho chuke hain. Main clear breakdown de raha hoon.

✅ Exam Module – Completed Features
1️⃣ Exam Management
✔ Add exam
✔ View exam list
✔ Edit exam
✔ Delete exam
✔ Course relation

URLs:

/exams/add/
/exams/list/
/exams/edit/<id>/
/exams/delete/<id>/
2️⃣ Result Management
✔ Add result
✔ Result list
✔ Edit result
✔ Delete result
✔ Student + Subject + Exam relation

URLs:

/exams/results/add/
/exams/results/list/
/exams/results/edit/<id>/
/exams/results/delete/<id>/
3️⃣ Result Card (Marksheet)
✔ Student result card
✔ Subject-wise marks
✔ Total marks
✔ Average marks
✔ Grade calculation
✔ Pass / Fail

URL:

/exams/student/<student_id>/
Example:

1) finance/models.py
from django.db import models
from students.models import Student


class Fee(models.Model):
    STATUS_CHOICES = [
        ('Paid', 'Paid'),
        ('Pending', 'Pending'),
        ('Partial', 'Partial'),
    ]

    student = models.ForeignKey(Student, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    total_fee = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2)
    paid_amount = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, default=0)
    payment_date = models.DateField()
    status = models.CharField(max_length=10, choices=STATUS_CHOICES, default='Pending')

    class Meta:
        ordering = ['-payment_date']

    def pending_amount(self):
        return self.total_fee - self.paid_amount

    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
        if self.paid_amount >= self.total_fee:
            self.status = 'Paid'
        elif self.paid_amount > 0:
            self.status = 'Partial'
        else:
            self.status = 'Pending'
        super().save(*args, **kwargs)

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.student} - {self.status}"
2) finance/forms.py
from django import forms
from .models import Fee


class FeeForm(forms.ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = Fee
        fields = ['student', 'total_fee', 'paid_amount', 'payment_date']
        widgets = {
            'student': forms.Select(attrs={'class': 'form-select'}),
            'total_fee': forms.NumberInput(attrs={
                'class': 'form-control',
                'placeholder': 'Enter total fee'
            }),
            'paid_amount': forms.NumberInput(attrs={
                'class': 'form-control',
                'placeholder': 'Enter paid amount'
            }),
            'payment_date': forms.DateInput(attrs={
                'type': 'date',
                'class': 'form-control'
            }),
        }

    def clean(self):
        cleaned_data = super().clean()
        total_fee = cleaned_data.get('total_fee')
        paid_amount = cleaned_data.get('paid_amount')

        if total_fee is not None and total_fee < 0:
            raise forms.ValidationError("Total fee cannot be negative.")

        if paid_amount is not None and paid_amount < 0:
            raise forms.ValidationError("Paid amount cannot be negative.")

        if total_fee is not None and paid_amount is not None:
            if paid_amount > total_fee:
                raise forms.ValidationError("Paid amount cannot be greater than total fee.")

        return cleaned_data
3) finance/views.py
from django.contrib import messages
from django.db.models import Q, Sum
from django.shortcuts import render, redirect, get_object_or_404
from .models import Fee
from .forms import FeeForm


def fee_add(request):
    form = FeeForm(request.POST or None)
    if form.is_valid():
        form.save()
        messages.success(request, "Fee record added successfully.")
        return redirect('fee_list')
    return render(request, 'finance/fee_form.html', {'form': form, 'title': 'Add Fee Record'})


def fee_list(request):
    query = request.GET.get('q', '')
    status = request.GET.get('status', '')

    fees = Fee.objects.select_related('student').all()

    if query:
        fees = fees.filter(
            Q(student__name__icontains=query)
        )

    if status:
        fees = fees.filter(status=status)

    context = {
        'fees': fees,
        'query': query,
        'selected_status': status,
        'statuses': ['Paid', 'Pending', 'Partial'],
    }
    return render(request, 'finance/fee_list.html', context)


def fee_edit(request, pk):
    fee = get_object_or_404(Fee, pk=pk)
    form = FeeForm(request.POST or None, instance=fee)
    if form.is_valid():
        form.save()
        messages.success(request, "Fee record updated successfully.")
        return redirect('fee_list')
    return render(request, 'finance/fee_form.html', {'form': form, 'title': 'Edit Fee Record'})


def fee_delete(request, pk):
    fee = get_object_or_404(Fee, pk=pk)
    if request.method == 'POST':
        fee.delete()
        messages.success(request, "Fee record deleted successfully.")
        return redirect('fee_list')
    return render(request, 'finance/fee_confirm_delete.html', {'fee': fee})


def student_fee_history(request, student_id):
    fees = Fee.objects.select_related('student').filter(student_id=student_id)

    student = None
    total_fee = 0
    total_paid = 0
    total_pending = 0

    if fees.exists():
        student = fees.first().student
        total_fee = fees.aggregate(total=Sum('total_fee'))['total'] or 0
        total_paid = fees.aggregate(total=Sum('paid_amount'))['total'] or 0
        total_pending = total_fee - total_paid

    context = {
        'student': student,
        'fees': fees,
        'total_fee': total_fee,
        'total_paid': total_paid,
        'total_pending': total_pending,
    }
    return render(request, 'finance/student_fee_history.html', context)
4) finance/urls.py
from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path('add/', views.fee_add, name='fee_add'),
    path('list/', views.fee_list, name='fee_list'),
    path('edit/<int:pk>/', views.fee_edit, name='fee_edit'),
    path('delete/<int:pk>/', views.fee_delete, name='fee_delete'),
    path('student/<int:student_id>/', views.student_fee_history, name='student_fee_history'),
]
5) finance/admin.py
from django.contrib import admin
from .models import Fee


@admin.register(Fee)
class FeeAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):
    list_display = ['id', 'student', 'total_fee', 'paid_amount', 'status', 'payment_date']
    list_filter = ['status', 'payment_date']
    search_fields = ['student__name']
Agar tumhare Student model me name field nahi hai, to student__name ko actual field name se replace karna padega.

</head>
<body>
<div class="container mt-5">
    <div class="card shadow p-4">
        <h2 class="mb-4">{{ title }}</h2>

        {% if messages %}
            {% for message in messages %}
                <div class="alert alert-success">{{ message }}</div>
            {% endfor %}
        {% endif %}

        {% if form.errors %}
            <div class="alert alert-danger">
                {{ form.errors }}
            </div>
        {% endif %}

        <form method="post">
            {% csrf_token %}
            {{ form.as_p }}
            <button type="submit" class="btn btn-primary">Save</button>
            <a href="{% url 'fee_list' %}" class="btn btn-secondary">Back</a>
        </form>
    </div>
</div>
</body>
</html>

                        <a href="{% url 'fee_delete' fee.id %}" class="btn btn-danger btn-sm">Delete</a>
                    </td>
                </tr>
                {% empty %}
                <tr>
                    <td colspan="9" class="text-center">No fee records found.</td>
                </tr>
                {% endfor %}
            </tbody>
        </table>
    </div>
</div>

Was fee.amount_paid renamed to fee.paid_amount (a DecimalField)? [y/N]  
Was fee.total_amount renamed to fee.total_fee (a DecimalField)? [y/N] y

It is impossible to change a nullable field 'payment_date' on fee to non-nullable without providing a default. This is because the database needs something to populate existing rows.
Please select a fix:
 1) Provide a one-off default now (will be set on all existing rows with a null value for this column)
 2) Ignore for now. Existing rows that contain NULL values will have to be handled manually, for example with a RunPython or RunSQL operation.
 3) Quit and manually define a default value in models.py.
Select an option: 

Ye Django ka normal migration prompt hai — koi problem nahi.

Tumhare case me error ka reason:

payment_date field pehle NULL allow karta tha

Ab model me wo required (non-nullable) ho gaya

Database me purane rows me NULL values hain

Isliye Django pooch raha hai un rows me kya value daale

Correct Option
👉 Option 1 select karo

Type karo:

1
Enter press karo.

Phir Django poochega default value
Prompt aayega:

Please enter the default value as valid Python.
Yaha ye type karo:

timezone.now
⚠️ quotes mat lagana.

Example:

>>> timezone.now
Ye kya karega
Database ke purane records me automatically:

payment_date = current date
set ho jayega.

New records me tum form se date select karoge.

1) core/views.py
Is file ko update karo:

from decimal import Decimal

from django.contrib.auth.decorators import login_required
from django.shortcuts import render

from students.models import Student
from faculty.models import Faculty
from courses.models import Course
from subjects.models import Subject
from attendance.models import Attendance
from exams.models import Exam, Result
from finance.models import Fee


@login_required
def dashboard(request):
    total_students = Student.objects.count()
    total_faculty = Faculty.objects.count()
    total_courses = Course.objects.count()
    total_subjects = Subject.objects.count()
    total_attendance = Attendance.objects.count()
    total_exams = Exam.objects.count()
    total_results = Result.objects.count()
    total_fees = Fee.objects.count()

    fee_records = Fee.objects.all()
    total_pending_fees = Decimal('0.00')

    for fee in fee_records:
        total_pending_fees += fee.pending_amount()

    if request.user.is_superuser:
        dashboard_title = "Admin Dashboard"
    elif getattr(request.user, "role", None) == "faculty":
        dashboard_title = "Faculty Dashboard"
    elif getattr(request.user, "role", None) == "student":
        dashboard_title = "Student Dashboard"
    else:
        dashboard_title = "Dashboard"

    context = {
        "dashboard_title": dashboard_title,
        "student_count": total_students,
        "faculty_count": total_faculty,
        "course_count": total_courses,
        "subject_count": total_subjects,
        "attendance_count": total_attendance,
        "exam_count": total_exams,
        "result_count": total_results,
        "fee_count": total_fees,
        "pending_fee_total": total_pending_fees,
    }

    return render(request, "core/dashboard.html", context)
2) core/urls.py
Agar file already hai to ensure ye route ho:

from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path('dashboard/', views.dashboard, name='dashboard'),
]
3) config/urls.py
Ensure core urls include ho rahe hon.

from django.contrib import admin
from django.urls import path, include

urlpatterns = [
    path('admin/', admin.site.urls),
    path('', include('core.urls')),
    path('exams/', include('exams.urls')),
    path('finance/', include('finance.urls')),
]
Agar tumhare baaki apps already included hain, to unhe rehne do. Bas core.urls ka include hona chahiye.

4) templates/core/dashboard.html
Ye full professional dashboard template use karo:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>{{ dashboard_title }}</title>
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
    <link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.3.3/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css" rel="stylesheet">
    <style>
        body {
            background-color: #f5f7fb;
        }
        .dashboard-header {
            margin-bottom: 30px;
        }
        .card-box {
            border: none;
            border-radius: 16px;
            box-shadow: 0 8px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.08);
            transition: transform 0.2s ease;
        }
        .card-box:hover {
            transform: translateY(-4px);
        }
        .stat-number {
            font-size: 2rem;
            font-weight: bold;
        }
        .quick-link a {
            text-decoration: none;
        }
    </style>
</head>

    <div class="dashboard-header text-center">

            </div>

                <h5>Total Subjects</h5>

            </div>
        </div>

        <div class="col-md-3">
            <div class="card card-box p-3">
                <h5>Attendance Records</h5>
                <div class="stat-number text-info">{{ attendance_count }}</div>
            </div>
        </div>

        <div class="col-md-3">
            <div class="card card-box p-3">
                <h5>Total Exams</h5>
                <div class="stat-number text-secondary">{{ exam_count }}</div>
            </div>

            </div>

        <div class="col-md-3">
            <div class="card card-box p-3">

                </p>

        </div>

                <div class="d-grid gap-2">
                    <a href="/students/list/" class="btn btn-outline-primary">Manage Students</a>
                    <a href="/faculty/list/" class="btn btn-outline-success">Manage Faculty</a>
                    <a href="/courses/list/" class="btn btn-outline-warning">Manage Courses</a>
                    <a href="/exams/list/" class="btn btn-outline-secondary">Manage Exams</a>
                    <a href="/finance/list/" class="btn btn-outline-danger">Manage Fees</a>
                </div>
            </div>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
</body>
</html>

Abhi kya working hai

Kya issue hai

/courses/list/ ❌ 404

Matlab tumhare actual faculty aur course URLs kuch aur hain.

Exact problem
Maine dashboard template me hardcoded links diye the:

/faculty/list/
/courses/list/
Lekin tumhare project me ye paths match nahi kar rahe.

Best fix
Dashboard template me hardcoded links hatao aur named URL tags use karo.

templates/core/dashboard.html
Quick Links wala part replace karo:

<div class="col-md-6">
    <div class="card card-box p-4 quick-link">
        <h4 class="mb-3">Quick Links</h4>
        <div class="d-grid gap-2">
            <a href="{% url 'student_list' %}" class="btn btn-outline-primary">Manage Students</a>
            <a href="{% url 'faculty_list' %}" class="btn btn-outline-success">Manage Faculty</a>
            <a href="{% url 'course_list' %}" class="btn btn-outline-warning">Manage Courses</a>
            <a href="{% url 'exam_list' %}" class="btn btn-outline-secondary">Manage Exams</a>
            <a href="{% url 'fee_list' %}" class="btn btn-outline-danger">Manage Fees</a>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
Important
Ye tabhi chalega jab tumhare URL names exactly ye hon:

student_list
faculty_list
course_list
exam_list
fee_list
Ab kya check karna hai
Apni faculty/urls.py ya faculties/urls.py me dekho:

name='faculty_list'
Aur courses/urls.py me dekho:

name='course_list'
Agar names different hain, to dashboard me wahi names use karne honge.

Example
Agar tumhare faculty app me ye hai:

path('list/', views.faculty_list, name='faculty_list')
to {% url 'faculty_list' %} sahi chalega.

Agar ye hai:

path('faculties/', views.faculty_list, name='faculties_list')
to dashboard me likhna padega:

<a href="{% url 'faculties_list' %}">
